meta.title: Chronicles of Georgia - Tourism Guide
meta.description: Independent tourism guide to the Chronicles of Georgia monument. Discover history, architecture, reviews, and tips for your visit.

hero.title: Chronicles of Georgia
hero.subtitle: Zurab Tsereteli's masterpiece - Georgia's historical monument
hero.tagline: The Dark Stonehenge of Tbilisi - A Soviet-era masterpiece that tells 3000 years of Georgian history

intro.title: Discover the Monument
intro.description: The Chronicles of Georgia is a giant historical monument located in the upper part of Tbilisi, in the Temqa district. Created by the famous Georgian sculptor Zurab Tsereteli in 1985.
highlights: Reliefs of Georgian kings and saints

photos.caption: Panoramic view of the monument at sunset
View of Tbilisi from the monument

reviews.title: Reviews from Real Visitors
reviews.declaration: Ratings and reviews sourced from Google Maps (last updated: 2026). We only display a selection of verified high-scoring reviews. For all complete and most recent reviews, click the link below.
reviews.moreLink: See all reviews on Google Maps

items.name: Nika K.
items.text: Amazing place! The view of Tbilisi is simply incredible. Every page of history is depicted in these columns.

items.name: Mariam B.
items.text: Must see! Zurab Tsereteli's creation is simply magnificent. The best place for taking photos.

items.name: David M.
items.text: Georgian Stonehenge - that's what foreigners call it. It truly deserves this name.

items.name: Anna C.
items.text: Perfect place for history lovers. Every column tells a story.

items.name: Thomas B.
items.text: Amazing Soviet-era monument with incredible views over Tbilisi. A must-visit for architecture enthusiasts.

items.name: Sofia L.
items.text: Very beautiful, but the location is a bit hard to find. But it's worth the search.

items.name: Alex R.
items.text: The Black Pillars of Tbilisi - perfect name for photography. Golden hour here is magical.

items.name: Natia G.
items.text: Tbilisi's calling card. Every Georgian should see it and we should show it to every foreigner.

visitDuration.title: How long does a visit take?
visitDuration.text: A visit to the monument takes about 1-2 hours. Allow extra time for taking photos and enjoying the views of Tbilisi.

worthIt.title: Is it worth visiting?
worthIt.text: Absolutely. The Chronicles of Georgia is one of the most amazing monuments in Tbilisi, depicting 3000 years of Georgian nation history.

whatInside.title: What can you find at the monument?
items: 30-meter columns with scenes from Georgian history
Reliefs of Georgian kings and saints
Depiction of the adoption of Christianity
Unforgettable views of Tbilisi and the Kura River

tips.title: Practical Tips
items: Visit at sunset for the best views and photos
Be careful in winter, it can be slippery
You can get there by bus, but taxi is the best option

map.title: How to Get There
map.address: QRC6+65V, Chronicles of Georgia Temqa, Tbilisi, Georgia

sources.title: Data and References
sources.text: This website is an independent third-party project for tourism information purposes. Information is sourced from public sources including Google Maps, local tourism authorities, and Wikipedia. Photos are sourced from Unsplash.
sources.disclaimer: We are not affiliated with the Municipality of Tbilisi or any other official body.
